stories.headline: McCormick visits Homer City data center site, pitches federal permit reform and 60-year nuclear licenses
stories.summary: Sen. Dave McCormick met with the Homer City project team Thursday and is sponsoring legislation that would force federal water permits to be granted or denied within one year, limit court challenges, and extend nuclear plant operating licenses from 40 to 60 years.

stories.headline: PA Senate votes 29-21 to end emissions inspections in 25 counties
stories.summary: Sen. Wayne Langerholc's SB 1298 cleared the Senate on Wednesday, exempting 25 counties — including Cumberland, Dauphin, Lancaster and York — from annual vehicle emissions testing; the bill now moves to the House.

stories.headline: Flynn, Lake spar over data centers in PA-22 Senate primary debate
stories.summary: Incumbent Sen. Marty Flynn and challenger Jeffrey Lake clashed Wednesday at a University of Scranton debate over data-center siting and energy demand ahead of the May 19 Democratic primary in the 22nd Senate District.

stories.headline: Fetterman: 'I haven't changed,' rules out switching parties
stories.summary: Sen. John Fetterman published an op-ed Thursday declaring he will not leave the Democratic Party despite expanding cross-aisle friendships with Senate Republicans as Democrats eye a Senate flip.

stories.headline: PJM forecasts sufficient generation for peak demand this summer
stories.summary: PJM said Thursday it expects enough capacity to meet projected summer peak across its 13-state footprint, with Friday's real-time load already at 82,506 MW against an 86,252 MW forecast peak and PJM-RTO LMP at $54.86/MWh.

stories.headline: Eversource CEO: 'We are resisting data centers'
stories.summary: Eversource's chief executive told investors the New England utility is pushing back against data-center interconnection requests, a sharp contrast with peers chasing the load — Western Hub cleared at $66.50/MWh in PJM Friday afternoon.

stories.headline: Evergy projects retail sales growth of up to 8% annually on data centers
stories.summary: Kansas-Missouri utility Evergy told analysts data-center load could lift retail sales as much as 8% per year, putting it among the most aggressive utility forecasts and underscoring the diverging strategies in the industry.

stories.headline: Sunrun Q1 sales drop sharply on solar tax-credit phase-out and tariffs
stories.summary: Residential solar leader Sunrun reported a steep first-quarter sales decline tied to the federal solar tax-credit phase-out and import tariffs, an early signal of how the rooftop market is absorbing the post-IRA reset.

stories.headline: Trump's $1B ballroom security request faces GOP resistance
stories.summary: House and Senate Republicans are privately objecting to including $1 billion for White House ballroom security in the immigration reconciliation bill, with PA Reps. Brian Fitzpatrick and Rob Bresnahan flagged as moderates to watch.

stories.headline: Schumer urges DHS to harden grids, water systems against AI-enabled hacks
stories.summary: Senate Minority Leader Chuck Schumer pressed DHS Secretary Markwayne Mullin in a May 7 letter to help state and local governments secure energy grids, water infrastructure and election systems against cyber risks from frontier AI models.

stories.headline: Trade court rules Trump's 10% global tariff illegal
stories.summary: A federal trade court ruled Thursday that Trump's justification for imposing a 10% global tariff after the Supreme Court struck down his original tariff authority is illegal, though the court declined to issue nationwide relief.

stories.headline: Pirates take series in Arizona behind Lowe HR, Keller's six strong
stories.summary: Brandon Lowe's 436-foot homer and Joey Bart's solo shot powered a 4-2 Pirates win over Arizona on Thursday as Mitch Keller (4-1) allowed two runs over six innings, giving Pittsburgh (21-17) the series 2-1 ahead of a weekend set at the Giants.

stories.headline: Penguins extend Dewar (2yr/$2.25M AAV) and Solovyov as offseason work begins
stories.summary: GM Kyle Dubas signed forward Connor Dewar to a two-year extension at $2.25M AAV and defenseman Ilya Solovyov to a one-year deal at $850K Thursday, with bigger calls on Malkin, Acciari and Mantha still ahead.

stories.headline: Steelers rookies report; Drew Allar takes the field Friday
stories.summary: Pittsburgh's 2026 rookie class — including QB Drew Allar, WR Germie Bernard and RB Eli Heidenreich — arrived at the UPMC Rooney Sports Complex Thursday for the start of rookie minicamp.
